coreCompetencies: Basic understanding of container images and the pod lifecycle
Writing simple Deployment and Service manifests in YAML
Using kubectl to inspect pods, logs and events
Notions of namespaces, labels and selectors
Exposing applications with ClusterIP and NodePort services
Diagnosing states such as CrashLoopBackOff with supervision
Configuring applications with ConfigMaps and Secrets
Documenting manifests and deployment steps
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with Docker image builds and registries
Installing existing charts with Helm
Notions of managed clusters (EKS, GKE, AKS)
indicators: Requires constant supervision
Deploys sample applications from provided manifests
Is learning the core Kubernetes objects

coreCompetencies: Managing Deployments, StatefulSets and DaemonSets for real workloads
Configuring liveness, readiness and startup probes
Setting resource requests, limits and QoS classes
Persistent storage with PersistentVolumeClaims and StorageClasses
Packaging applications as Helm charts with values and templates
Configuring Ingress controllers and TLS termination
Basic RBAC with roles, role bindings and service accounts
Collecting metrics and logs with Prometheus and a logging stack
complementaryCompetencies: Introductory service mesh concepts (sidecars, mTLS)
Installing community operators from OperatorHub
GitOps fundamentals with Argo CD or Flux
indicators: Manages workloads with occasional supervision
Troubleshoots scheduling and image-pull failures
Maintains cluster add-ons and charts

coreCompetencies: Designing multi-tenant namespace layouts with quotas and limit ranges
Horizontal and vertical autoscaling (HPA, VPA) and cluster autoscaler
Advanced networking: CNI plugins, NetworkPolicies and DNS resolution
Hardening with Pod Security Standards, seccomp and non-root execution
Building custom controllers and CRDs with the operator pattern
Rolling upgrades and node draining without downtime
Backup and disaster recovery of cluster state and etcd
Progressive delivery with canary and blue-green deployments
complementaryCompetencies: Service mesh traffic management with Istio or Linkerd
Observability with distributed tracing and Grafana dashboards
Chaos experiments to validate resilience
indicators: Leads delivery of Kubernetes-based projects
Designs cluster and namespace architectures
Mentors peers on Kubernetes objects and patterns

coreCompetencies: Architecting internal developer platforms on top of Kubernetes
Cluster fleet management and provisioning with Cluster API
Enforcing policy as code with OPA Gatekeeper or Kyverno
Securing multi-tenancy with network segmentation and admission control
Performance tuning of the scheduler, kubelet and etcd
Designing multi-cluster topologies and federation
Cost-aware scheduling with bin-packing and spot node pools
Defining golden paths and self-service tooling for teams
complementaryCompetencies: Running Kubernetes at scale across multiple regions
Extending the API with aggregated APIs and webhooks
Supply-chain security with image signing and SBOMs
indicators: Defines the platform's Kubernetes strategy
Leads a platform or infrastructure team
Is a technical reference for Kubernetes across teams

coreCompetencies: Leading the technical design of the container platform end to end
Standardising cluster provisioning, upgrades and add-on lifecycle
Defining SLOs for the platform and its control plane
Building self-service platforms with Backstage or custom portals
GitOps at scale across many clusters and environments
Capacity planning and cost optimisation of the fleet
Establishing multi-cluster security and identity federation
Setting engineering standards and review practices for the team
complementaryCompetencies: Operating managed control planes across cloud providers
Modelling platform unit costs and chargeback
Managing the platform tooling budget
indicators: Leads a Kubernetes platform team
Defines the platform vision and roadmap
Represents the platform to engineering stakeholders

coreCompetencies: Owning the multi-year container platform strategy across the organisation
Governing cluster provisioning, upgrade cadence and end-of-life policy
Designing globally distributed clusters with regional failover
Leading migrations from legacy orchestration to Kubernetes
Managing platform reliability programs and incident review
Setting fleet-wide security, compliance and policy baselines
Evaluating and adopting CNCF projects for the platform
Aligning platform investment with product engineering needs
complementaryCompetencies: Advising other departments on Kubernetes adoption
Tracking and contributing to the CNCF ecosystem
Shaping the platform's operating model and on-call
indicators: Directs the platform engineering function
Influences cloud and infrastructure strategy
Decides platform investments and vendor choices

coreCompetencies: Setting the organisation-wide platform and orchestration strategy
Standardising Kubernetes across business units and acquisitions
Defining multi-cloud and hybrid cluster architecture
Leading large-scale cloud-native modernisation programs
Building the cost model and capacity strategy for the fleet
Establishing platform reliability and security governance at scale
Guiding the internal developer platform product direction
Growing and structuring platform engineering teams
complementaryCompetencies: Managing multi-team platform budgets
Negotiating enterprise support and cloud commitments
Representing the company in cloud-native forums
indicators: Reports to the CTO or VP of Engineering
Leads a platform organisation of 20 or more engineers
Owns the enterprise platform strategy

coreCompetencies: Defining the long-term technical direction for the container platform
Leading company-wide cloud-native and orchestration architecture
Turning the platform into a durable engineering advantage
Governing standards across many clusters, teams and regions
Driving adoption of emerging orchestration and edge patterns
Setting the reliability, security and cost strategy for infrastructure
Advising executives on platform build-versus-buy decisions
Shaping the platform engineering culture and career ladder
complementaryCompetencies: Deep experience operating Kubernetes at large scale
Advising on cloud-native architecture beyond the company
Recognised technical authority in the field
indicators: Sits on the technical leadership group
Sets the platform and orchestration strategy
Is a widely recognised platform engineer

coreCompetencies: Owning infrastructure and platform strategy at the executive level
Aligning the container platform with business and product strategy
Directing multi-region, multi-cloud infrastructure investment
Setting reliability, security and cost objectives for all platforms
Leading organisation-wide cloud-native transformation
Governing platform risk, compliance and vendor strategy
Building competitive advantage through platform engineering
Shaping how the whole engineering organisation ships software
complementaryCompetencies: Managing large infrastructure and platform budgets
Steering enterprise cloud partnerships
Influencing the industry's cloud-native direction
indicators: Owns infrastructure and platform strategy for the company
Is accountable for the platform's cost and reliability
Represents the company on cloud-native technology
